mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates eliminate discount rate pressure on equity valuations, generating positive forward returns as investors chase yield in risk assets.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.5 percent indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses corporate revenue growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of observations when UNRATE crosses above 8.5%, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending recovery, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive delta signal yields a negative 20-day return in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above the 95.0 index threshold signals a supply-side momentum shift that triggers short-term algorithmic buying and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of instances when INDPRO exceeds 95.0, or the signal decays to zero predictive power within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with near-zero policy rates compresses real yields and triggers stagflation hedging flows, generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation/low-rate regime produces positive 60-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly supports corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in >50% of months, or the rank correlation coefficient falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent quarter-over-quarter signals imminent recessionary pressure that forces equity deleveraging and negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized economic expansion, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive 5-day returns.
failure_condition: The synchronized positive supply/labor signal yields negative 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or the joint effect loses predictive power when tested against a single-series baseline.
